mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rate increases discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuations and leading to negative 20-day forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds its 12-month moving average, failing to confirm the discount rate compression effect.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market slack that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE exceeds 5.0% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, contradicting the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls confirm robust consumer demand and income growth, driving positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when both series increase MoM, falsifying the joint positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth (year-over-year > 2%) indicates supply-side expansion that boosts corporate profits and equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of months when INDPRO YoY > 2%, indicating the regime does not reliably produce positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect strong consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth and leads to positive short-term equity returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when RSAFS increases month-over-month, suggesting the relationship is not robust.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in industrial production (>0.5%) signals accelerating supply-side activity that lifts equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of months when the trigger holds, failing to show the predicted positive return.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 3% and payroll growth slows to under 100k per month, tight monetary policy coincides with weakening labor demand, amplifying downward pressure on equities over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances when both conditions hold, contradicting the joint negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1,500 thousand units annualized reflect a robust construction sector that contributes positively to GDP and equity returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when HOUST exceeds 1,500k, indicating the regime does not support positive returns.
